name: Mixture of Experts (MoE)
summary: An architecture where a model consists of multiple specialized sub-networks ('experts') and a gating mechanism that routes each input to a subset of experts. This allows models to have very large total parameter counts while only activating a fraction of them for any given input, dramatically improving computational efficiency. MoE enables models like Mixtral and reportedly GPT-4 to achieve frontier performance at a fraction of the inference cost of dense models of equivalent capability.
query_hooks: How can AI models be more efficient?
